title: Error when attempting to compose LXD VMs:  this value is less than or equal to 0
description:
- MAAS Version: 3.6.2 (snap, 24.04)
- Tested on both Virsh and LXD based KVM pods.
- Likely a continuation/regression of previous bugs, but was suggested a new bug be opened:
  https://bugs.launchpad.net/maas/+bug/2048399

From what I gathered, in the database, some kind of error is occurring in the core/memory count calculation which is leading to the total cores and memory to be read as zero. I suspect this is causing validation to fail as MAAS thinks there are not enough cores to satisfy the VM creation request.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Add a LXD host as a KVM pod.
2. Make sure there's ample resources on the host (RAM, CPU, storage)
3. Click refresh host to ensure hypervisor info is up-to-date
4. Crank the over-commit ratio to max for good measure
5. Attempt to compose a VM in the UI:  Ensure this value is less than or equal to 0.

Expected:
VM is created

Actual:
A field validation error is shown in the UI, with the familiar message:

Error shown in journald reveals it to be it not liking the available core count: https://paste.ubuntu.com/p/6pwHrM7hMv/plain/

On closer inspection, "maas admin vm-hosts read" reveals the possible issue, the resource counts are zeroed out or otherwise wrong:

        "name": "supermicro",
        "total": {
            "cores": 0,
            "memory": 0,
            "local_storage": 7610403520512
        },

Actual resources on the host:
lab@supermicro:~$ free -h
               total        used        free      shared  buff/cache   available
Mem:           251Gi       170Gi        67Gi        34Gi        50Gi        80Gi
Swap:          8.0Gi       2.5Gi       5.5Gi

NUMA:
  NUMA node(s):           1
  NUMA node0 CPU(s):      0-127

There are only 7 VMs running on the host and none of them have enough allocation to exhaust the free count of cores:

maas=# select * from maasserver_virtualmachine;
 id  |            created            |            updated            |    identifier    | pinned_cores | unpinned_cores | memory | hugepages_backed | bmc_id | machine_id | project
-----+-------------------------------+-------------------------------+------------------+--------------+----------------+--------+------------------+--------+------------+---------
 309 | 2025-12-02 13:49:23.109118-06 | 2025-12-04 12:12:19.691593-06 | sought-koala     | {}           |              8 |  16384 | f                |     65 |        472 | maas
 308 | 2025-12-02 13:49:18.233601-06 | 2025-12-04 12:12:19.742377-06 | super-grub       | {}           |              8 |  16384 | f                |     65 |        471 | maas
 267 | 2025-10-30 14:14:46.682895-05 | 2025-12-04 12:12:26.216767-06 | maas             | {}           |             32 |  15258 | f                |     64 |            | default
 268 | 2025-10-30 14:14:46.698467-05 | 2025-12-04 12:12:26.259826-06 | atuin            | {}           |              8 |  15258 | f                |     64 |        421 | maas
 270 | 2025-10-30 14:14:46.70922-05  | 2025-12-04 12:12:26.288503-06 | jujucontroller-1 | {}           |              8 |  15258 | f                |     64 |        423 | maas
 271 | 2025-10-30 14:14:46.714784-05 | 2025-12-04 12:12:26.314193-06 | jujucontroller-2 | {}           |              8 |  15258 | f                |     64 |        424 | maas
 272 | 2025-10-30 14:14:46.719993-05 | 2025-12-04 12:12:26.344029-06 | jujucontroller-3 | {}           |              8 |  15258 | f                |     64 |        425 | maas

 especially with over-commit ration maxed out, from the UI:

CPU cores Allocated
32
Others
32
Free
1216

messages.content:
- MAAS Version: 3.6.2 (snap, 24.04)
- Tested on both Virsh and LXD based KVM pods.
- Likely a continuation/regression of previous bugs, but was suggested a new bug be opened:
  https://bugs.launchpad.net/maas/+bug/2048399

Steps to reproduce:
1. Add a LXD host as a KVM pod. 
2. Make sure there's ample resources on the host (RAM, CPU, storage)
3. Click refresh host to ensure hypervisor info is up-to-date
4. Attempt to compose a VM in the UI:  Ensure this value is less than or equal to 0.

Expected:
VM is created

Actual:
A field validation error is shown in the UI, with the familiar message:

Error shown in journald reveals it to be it not liking the available core count: https://paste.ubuntu.com/p/6pwHrM7hMv/plain/

On closer inspection, "maas admin vm-hosts read" reveals the possible issue, the resource counts are zeroed out or otherwise wrong:

        "name": "supermicro",
        "total": {
            "cores": 0,
            "memory": 0,
            "local_storage": 7610403520512
        },

Actual resources on the host:
lab@supermicro:~$ free -h
               total        used        free      shared  buff/cache   available
Mem:           251Gi       170Gi        67Gi        34Gi        50Gi        80Gi
Swap:          8.0Gi       2.5Gi       5.5Gi

NUMA:
  NUMA node(s):           1
  NUMA node0 CPU(s):      0-127


There are only 7 VMs running on the host and none of them have enough allocation to exhaust the free count of cores, especially with over-commit ration maxed out, from the UI:

CPU cores Allocated
32
Others
32
Free
1216

Hi Dagmawi,

Could you run `maas $profile vm-host refresh $id_of_vm_host` and share here the output plus all the logs that you can gather from MAAS? The problem is that MAAS cannot get the cores and RAM of your VM Host. In most of the cases this is not a MAAS bug. Instead it can be:

- wrong LXD or Virsh credentials
- inability to access LXD or Virsh power_address
- your newly added VM host has many interfaces and the commissioning scripts that MAAS runs to identify its network characteristics are super slow to finish.

In general, when you register a VM host this is what is happening: MAAS is creating a VM host object and a twin fake MAAS machine.Then, with your provided LXD/Virsh credentials is getting the machine characteristics. Then it is putting them as an output of a commissioning script, so, in practice, it is faking a real machine commissioning. Then it automatically runs VM host refresh on the VM host object.

The entire logic of VM host refresh, is to find the machine object of VM Host in MAAS and match it with the VM host object. So for every VM Host we have those two objects. During refresh, MAAS is trying to make the match with the IP provided in the VM Host power_address. Question: could you also check this address and at the same time, find the relevant MAAS machine and observe its network page? 

If for any reason, the MAAS machine object does not have this IP, then the VM host quotas are not updated at all.

Hi Stamatis,

The MAAS server does have connectivity to the LXD node and I can confirm this by the way of doing "Refresh host" which does return without errors. I can also see that the certificate configured for MAAS in lxd and LXD's trust store match fingerprints:

MAAS:
    CN
    supermicro.maas@maas
    Expiration date
    Sun, 02 Dec. 2035 22:42:37
    Fingerprint
    32:DA:CD:6F:4B:89:18:D7:D5:3D:AC:F4:E7:91:46:04:5D:85:30:0E:E7:69:39:2E:87:EC:C9:19:87:D6:84:85

LXD:
 lxc config trust ls
+--------+-------+----------------------+--------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
|  TYPE  | NAME  |     COMMON NAME      | FINGERPRINT  |          ISSUE DATE          |         EXPIRY DATE          |
+--------+-------+----------------------+--------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| client | stdin | supermicro.maas@maas | 32dacd6f4b89 | Dec 4, 2025 at 10:42pm (UTC) | Dec 2, 2035 at 10:42pm (UTC) |


I do see though that the machine object for the KVM pod that's added shows a failed commissioning though it is successfully in a deployed state in MAAS.


refresh host output: https://paste.ubuntu.com/p/5HbwSMy5jy/

Thank you for performing the sanity checks for me. Would you mind sharing the commissioning script outputs of the fake machine? Both 20-maas-03-machine-resources and 50-maas-01-commissioning?

And also a screenshot with the networking tab of the fake machine. I have a feeling that the IP you have in the power address of the vm host is not detected as an IP of one of the machine interfaces

Hi Stamatis,

Thanks for pointing me in the right direction.  50-maas-01-commissioning seems to be the culprit. Unfortunately the UI doesn't provide an output of the script results but from the MAAS logs I see the following:
----
Dec 23 11:56:05 maas maas-regiond[1697]:           File "/snap/maas/40614/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/django/db/models/base.py", line 1502, in full_clean
Dec 23 11:56:05 maas maas-regiond[1697]:             raise ValidationError(errors)
Dec 23 11:56:05 maas maas-regiond[1697]:         django.core.exceptions.ValidationError: {'vlan': ['VLAN interface requires connection to a VLAN.']}
Dec 23 11:56:05 maas maas-regiond[1697]:

Here's the full JSON of the 50-maas-01-commissioning output:
https://pastebin.ubuntu.com/p/5jBty2yKK6/

I tried entirely deleting the machine from MAAS and then adding it directly as a LXD VM host.
What seems to happen is as follows:

1. MAAS adds the LXD host successfully and syncs the CPU/memory/storage data just for the VM host object

2. MAAS in addition seems to try to do an in-place commissioning using the commissioning scripts. 

3. Since this is an already-running machine it has a lot of veths, bridges, vlans and physical interfaces on it. It seems something is being parsed incorrectly around here and it fails silently.  There is a vlan interface type somewhere in here that it's not liking. 

My netplan config for this machine is as follows: https://paste.ubuntu.com/p/m7gpXDcnKj/

title: smartctl.py fails on some RAID volumes that dont support self_test, making requiring someone to manually override
description:
\Describe the bug: 
on RAID volumes (hardware RAID) there are occasions where the LUN does not support SMART self-test.  Because of this commissioning fails and the machine is unusable.  

This requires a manual override in order to make the machine usable again.

smartctl.py should be "smart" enough to NOT fail if a desired action is not supported.  Rather it should note the lack of self-test in the results and move on from there.

Steps to reproduce: 
commission the machine "cajal" in the lab
Note the commissioning fails with smart validation due to lack of self-test support


Expected behavior (what should have happened?):
Smartctl.py should recognize that self-test is not supported by this device, and should accept that and move on, rather than failing due to something that cannot be changed (e.g. a lack of support for a given feature) vs a legitimate failure where self-test returned bad results.

Actual behavior (what actually happened?): 
Cajal is not commissioned successfully and someone then has to spend time investigating (in this case two someones) in order to figure out that, ultimately, self-test is simply not supported.



MAAS version and installation type (deb, snap): 

3.5.10 - whatever version you're installing on the edge cloud, I presume snaps.

MAAS setup (HA, single node, multiple regions/racks):

HA

Host OS distro and version:

Ubuntu... 24.04?

Additional context:
This is the output from a recent failed run:

INFO: Verifying SMART support for the following drive: /dev/disk/by-id/wwn-0x600508b1001c7b0b20f8983eacb35e76
INFO: Running command: sudo -n smartctl --all /dev/disk/by-id/wwn-0x600508b1001c7b0b20f8983eacb35e76
INFO: SMART support is available; continuing...
INFO: Running command: sudo -n smartctl -t short /dev/disk/by-id/wwn-0x600508b1001c7b0b20f8983eacb35e76
ERROR: Failed to start smartctl self-test: short
INFO: Verifying SMART data on /dev/disk/by-id/wwn-0x600508b1001c7b0b20f8983eacb35e76
INFO: Running command: sudo -n smartctl --xall /dev/disk/by-id/wwn-0x600508b1001c7b0b20f8983eacb35e76
SUCCESS: SMART validation has PASSED for: /dev/disk/by-id/wwn-0x600508b1001c7b0b20f8983eacb35e76
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
smartctl 7.2 2020-12-30 r5155 [x86_64-linux-5.15.0-164-generic] (local build)
Copyright (C) 2002-20, Bruce Allen, Christian Franke, www.smartmontools.org

=== START OF INFORMATION SECTION ===
Vendor:               HPE
Product:              LOGICAL VOLUME
Revision:             1.99
Compliance:           SPC-3
User Capacity:        12,000,105,070,592 bytes [12.0 TB]
Logical block size:   512 bytes
Physical block size:  4096 bytes
Logical Unit id:      0x600508b1001c7b0b20f8983eacb35e76
Serial number:        PEYHB0DRHCU00K
Device type:          disk
Local Time is:        Mon Jan 26 16:23:58 2026 UTC
SMART support is:     Available - device has SMART capability.
SMART support is:     Enabled

title: Sometimes disks in Linux are not named correctly
description:
Describe the bug: 
We are deploying a set of 30+ machines with Testflinger. To make automation easier we name two disks in MAAS as disk0 and disk1. However sometimes disk1 is not present in Linux.
Here is an example, the same server, two deployments in sequence

*********************************************
* Starting testflinger test phase on ledian *
*********************************************
2026-03-30 11:04:15,996 ledian INFO: DEVICE CONNECTOR: BEGIN testrun
total 0
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 13 Mar 30 11:00 disk0 -> ../../nvme0n1
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 15 Mar 30 11:00 disk0-part1 -> ../../nvme0n1p1
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 15 Mar 30 11:00 disk0-part2 -> ../../nvme0n1p2
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 15 Mar 30 11:00 disk0-part3 -> ../../nvme0n1p3
2026-03-30 11:04:16,480 ledian INFO: DEVICE CONNECTOR: END testrun

*********************************************
* Starting testflinger test phase on ledian *
*********************************************
2026-03-30 12:30:03,423 ledian INFO: DEVICE CONNECTOR: BEGIN testrun
total 0
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 13 Mar 30 12:27 disk0 -> ../../nvme0n1
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 15 Mar 30 12:27 disk0-part1 -> ../../nvme0n1p1
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 15 Mar 30 12:27 disk0-part2 -> ../../nvme0n1p2
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 15 Mar 30 12:27 disk0-part3 -> ../../nvme0n1p3
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root  9 Mar 30 12:27 disk1 -> ../../sda
lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 10 Mar 30 12:27 disk1-part1 -> ../../sda1
2026-03-30 12:30:03,908 ledian INFO: DEVICE CONNECTOR: END testrun


Steps to reproduce: 
Name disks in MAAS differently than sda, nvme etc
Deploy enough times and maybe it will happen. I found a server (ledian) which can hit it 1/5 times, but also not guaranteed



Expected behavior (what should have happened?):
Consisten disk naming as seen in MAAS UI



Actual behavior (what actually happened?): 

Sometimes disk1 is not present



MAAS version and installation type (deb, snap): 
3.7 snap
I never noticed this on 3.5

title: Unable to commission new machines unless fabrics are pristine
description:
Describe the bug: 
Unless the fabrics from an earlier commissioning are cleaned up, the 50-maas-01-commissioning script fails during post processing with the error:

maasserver.models.interface.MultipleObjectsReturned: get() returned more than one PhysicalInterface -- it returned 2!

This makes it very difficult to commission a large number of servers.

Steps to reproduce: 

I found that in MAAS 3.7.1 this would occur whenever you commission more than 2 servers at a time, but at least you didn't have to cleanup the fabrics in between.

With MAAS 3.7.2 I now have to commission 2 servers, then clean up the bogus fabrics, then commission another 2 servers, etc. So to reproduce this issue in MAAS 3.7.2:
* Commissioning a couple servers that have multiple NICs (ours have 4), so that it results in a set of random fabrics. Wait for this to complete and note that in a pristine environment, 50-maas-01-commissioning succeeds.
* Then commissioning another couple servers
* This second set fails on 50-maas-01-commissioning with the error noted above.


Expected behavior (what should have happened?):

Commissioning should succeed.


Actual behavior (what actually happened?): 

Commissioning fails on 50-maas-01-commissioning. Most like due to the use of get_or_create() in the script, which is known to be prone to such issues (see #1988976).

MAAS version and installation type (deb, snap): 

Version 3.7.2 (3.7.2-17972-g.35e297c4d) installed via snap


MAAS setup (HA, single node, multiple regions/racks):

HA (2 combined region+rack controllers, 1 separate database server) 


Host OS distro and version:

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS


Additional context:

Full stack trace

Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]: metadataserver.api: [critical] bl-r2-c3-16.glo.bm.netzerocompute.com(7pachw): commissioning script '50-maas-01-commissioning' failed during post-processing.
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:         Traceback (most recent call last):
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/metadataserver/api.py", line 885, in signal
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             target_status = process(node, request, status)
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/metadataserver/api.py", line 689, in _process_commissioning
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             self._store_results(
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/metadataserver/api.py", line 571, in _store_results
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             script_result.store_result(
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/maasserver/models/scriptresult.py", line 374, in store_result
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             signal_status = try_or_log_event(
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:         --- <exception caught here> ---
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/metadataserver/api.py", line 487, in try_or_log_event
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             func(*args, **kwargs)
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/metadataserver/builtin_scripts/hooks.py", line 1221, in process_lxd_results
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             _process_lxd_resources(node, data)
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/metadataserver/builtin_scripts/hooks.py", line 691, in _process_lxd_resources
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             network_devices = update_node_network_information(node, data, numa_nodes)
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/metadataserver/builtin_scripts/hooks.py", line 241, in update_node_network_information
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             update_node_interfaces(node, data)
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/provisioningserver/utils/twisted.py", line 203, in wrapper
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             result = func(*args, **kwargs)
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/maasserver/utils/orm.py", line 776, in call_within_transaction
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             return func_within_txn(*args, **kwargs)
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/usr/lib/python3.12/contextlib.py", line 81, in inner
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:             return func(*args, **kwds)
Apr 10 12:13:08 maas-1 maas-regiond[1064]:           File "/snap/maas/41649/lib/python3.12/site-packages/metadataserver/builtin_scripts/network.py", line 118, in update_node_interfaces

messages.content:
Describe the bug: 
Unless the fabrics from an earlier commissioning are cleaned up, the 50-maas-01-commissioning script fails during post processing with the error:

maasserver.models.interface.MultipleObjectsReturned: get() returned more than one PhysicalInterface -- it returned 2!

This makes it very difficult to commission a large number of servers.

Steps to reproduce: 

I found that in MAAS 3.7.1 this would occur whenever you commission more than 2 servers at a time, but at least you didn't have to cleanup the fabrics in between.

With MAAS 3.7.2 I now have to commission 2 servers, then clean up the bogus fabrics, then commission another 2 servers, etc. So to reproduce this issue in MAAS 3.7.2:
* Commissioning a couple servers that have multiple NICs (ours have 4), so that it results in a set of random fabrics. Wait for this to complete and note that in a pristine environment, 50-maas-01-commissioning succeeds.
* Then commissioning another couple servers
* This second set fails on 50-maas-01-commissioning with the error noted above.


Expected behavior (what should have happened?):

Commissioning should succeed.


Actual behavior (what actually happened?): 

Commissioning fails on 50-maas-01-commissioning. Most like due to the use of get_or_create() in the script, which is known to be prone to such issues (see #1988976).

MAAS version and installation type (deb, snap): 

Version 3.7.2 (3.7.2-17972-g.35e297c4d) installed via snap


MAAS setup (HA, single node, multiple regions/racks):

HA (2 combined region+rack controllers, 1 separate database server) 


Host OS distro and version:

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS


Additional context:

Full stack trace

title: Wortmann TERRA SERVER 7120 G3 commissioning switches power type from Redfish to IPMI when SMBIOS DMI type 42 is missing
description:
On Wortmann_AG TERRA_SERVER (System Version/Model: R1208WFTYSR; baseboard Intel S2600WFT), a machine manually configured in MAAS to use Redfish for power control is switched back to IPMI after commissioning. During commissioning, the built-in script 30-maas-01-bmc-config attempts Redfish Host Interface auto-detection via SMBIOS/DMI type 42, fails with “Missing SMBIOS data” (because the platform exposes no DMI type 42 records), then detects/configures IPMI. IPMI power control then fails in this environment because MAAS cannot reach IPMI (firewall).
The plaftorm description website: https://www.wortmann.de/en-gb/content/terraserver-7000_serie_g3.aspx

Steps to reproduce:

    Add/enlist the server Wortmann_AG TERRA_SERVER R1208WFTYSR into MAAS.
    In MAAS UI/API, set the machine Power type = Redfish and configure valid Redfish address/credentials (out-of-band BMC management NIC). Confirm power actions work via Redfish.
    Commission the machine.
    After commissioning completes, check the machine’s power configuration / attempt a power action.

Expected behavior (what should have happened?):

    Commissioning should not change an explicitly user-configured power type (Redfish).
    If Redfish Host Interface (SMBIOS type 42) is missing, MAAS should record that auto-detection is unavailable and leave existing power settings intact (or only change power type when explicitly requested / when an opt-in setting is enabled).

Actual behavior (what actually happened?):

    30-maas-01-bmc-config output shows:
        Checking for Redfish...
        Redfish detection and configuration failed. Reason: Missing SMBIOS data
        then falls back to IPMI: IPMI detected! and configures an IPMI user (maas).
    After commissioning, the machine’s power configuration is effectively switched to IPMI, and power control fails because MAAS cannot reach IPMI due to firewalling.

MAAS version and installation type (deb, snap):

    MAAS version: <fill in>
    Installation type: <deb|snap>

MAAS setup (HA, single node, multiple regions/racks):

    single node

Host OS distro and version:

    Ubuntu 24.04, 
    MAAS snap: installed:          3.7.2-17972-g.35e297c4d                   (41649) 235MB -

Additional context:

    Hardware identification from dmidecode:
        System Information: Manufacturer Wortmann_AG, Product Name TERRA_SERVER, Version/Model R1208WFTYSR
        Base Board: Intel Corporation S2600WFT (Version H48104-872)
        BMC-related DMI includes DMI type 38: IPMI Device Information (IPMI Spec Version 2.0)
    Redfish Host Interface SMBIOS check on this server returns no entries:
        Output of sudo dmidecode -t 42:

        # dmidecode 3.3
        Getting SMBIOS data from sysfs.
        SMBIOS 2.8 present.

    The Redfish detection path in MAAS relies on dmidecode -t 42 and searching for Protocol ID: 04 (Redfish over IP); absence leads to “Missing SMBIOS data” and fallback behavior. (See MAAS code: src/metadataserver/builtin_scripts/commissioning_scripts/bmc_config.py around Redfish _get_smbios_data() / _detect().)
    Environment blocks IPMI access from MAAS (firewall), but Redfish out-of-band access works; commissioning causes regression by reverting to IPMI.

Relevant MAAS code indicates Redfish auto-detection relies on parsing dmidecode -t 42 and looking for Protocol ID: 04 (Redfish over IP); if absent it raises "Missing SMBIOS data" and falls back to IPMI.

    https://github.com/canonical/maas/blob/60d5994a23f2d2ad988fa8cdef1cb8cb41dc0b52/src/metadataserver/builtin_scripts/commissioning_scripts/bmc_config.py#L972-L1034
